evidence_quotes.quote: Current practice allows publishers to withdraw video games from the market and disable / destroy them, after they've been legally purchased.

paragraphs.text: Liberty, equality, fraternity As a proud European citizen I strongly believe in the three basic values which have led the EU to being the best place to live in the XXI century. That's why I'd like to propose adding another extremely important issue to the rooster of those already proposed under the Digital Fairness Act - ensuring legislation that will protect our digital consumers rights in regard to destroying video games - vital parts of modern art and culture. Current practice allows publishers to withdraw video games from the market and disable / destroy them, after they've been legally purchased. This is outrageously unfair and unprecedented. Hundreds of unique games were already devoured by the unchecked corporate greed and malice, robbing thousands of players of their memories and digital "homes". It cannot go on like this. Gamers all over the Europe have already voiced their concerns and need to be heard. We have the liberty to experience our favorite games for as long as we like. We demand to be treated equally with other retail consumers and not left on the mercy of the multi-billion industry. We are speaking as a one voice, united by our shared love for the video games. Stop killing games!
